summary: A Go-based CLI tool (GOrchestra) that migrates, detects, and runs Goss configurations with OpenTelemetry tracing and a plugin system.
concerns: Heavy reliance on test files for understanding core logic suggests implementation may be scattered
Migration path (goss translator) might carry legacy constraints
Multiple recent metadata/documentation commits could indicate instability in project direction
Missing key implementation files in hot files list (e.g., reporter.go is inferred, not provided)
suggestions: Focus on runner.go and translator.go to understand core value proposition
Review the OpenTelemetry multi-backend reporter added in recent commits
Examine the schema validation logic for extensibility
Check plugin system for future integration capabilities
